Hibernate Annotation Configuration Concepts

1. @Entity

* @Entity indicates that the class is a Hibernate/JPA entity.
* Hibernate maps this class to a database table.

Example:
@Entity
public class Employee {
}

2. @Table

* @Table specifies the database table name.
* It maps the entity class to a table.

Example:
@Entity
@Table(name="employee")
public class Employee {
}

3. @Id

* @Id specifies the primary key of the table.

Example:
@Id
private int id;

4. @GeneratedValue

* @GeneratedValue automatically generates primary key values.
* Commonly used with AUTO, IDENTITY, SEQUENCE, or TABLE strategies.

Example:
@Id
@GeneratedValue
private int id;

5. @Column

* @Column maps a class field to a database column.

Example:
@Column(name="first_name")
private String firstName;

6. Hibernate Configuration File (hibernate.cfg.xml)

The hibernate.cfg.xml file contains database connection and Hibernate settings.

Example:

<hibernate-configuration>
 <session-factory>

  <property name="hibernate.dialect">
   org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect
  </property>

  <property name="hibernate.connection.driver_class">
   com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver
  </property>

  <property name="hibernate.connection.url">
   jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/company
  </property>

  <property name="hibernate.connection.username">
   root
  </property>

  <property name="hibernate.connection.password">
   root
  </property>

 </session-factory>
</hibernate-configuration>

7. Dialect

* Dialect tells Hibernate which database is being used.
* Generates database-specific SQL statements.

Example:
org.hibernate.dialect.MySQLDialect

8. Driver

* JDBC Driver used to connect Java and database.

Example:
com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

9. Connection URL

* Specifies database location and schema.

Example:
jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/company

10. Username

* Database username.

Example:
root

11. Password

* Database password.

Example:
root

Hibernate Annotation Flow

Entity Class
|
v
Annotations
(@Entity, @Table, @Id, @Column)
|
v
Hibernate Configuration
(hibernate.cfg.xml)
|
v
SessionFactory
|
v
Session
|
v
Database Operations
|
v
Commit / Rollback
